what_it_does: Implements a focus/attention mechanism where one object becomes the subject and everything else recedes (desaturated, dimmed, slightly blurred and lowered) while the subject lifts forward (translateY, scale, raised shadow and z-index). Defines recede/focus tuning tokens, a .focus-scope container with transitions, explicit [data-recede] and [data-focus] targeting, an implicit is-focusing mode that recedes everything that is not the subject, and a reduced-motion fallback.
observations: Receding applies opacity + saturate + blur filter; the subject lifts via translateY+scale, --elev-4 shadow, --z-raised, position:relative
Implicit mode uses :not([data-focus]):not(:has([data-focus])) so containers wrapping the subject do not recede
prefers-reduced-motion:reduce drops the subject transform and keeps only desaturation (no blur) for receded items
@kind annotations on the unitless tuning tokens
standouts: Framed as the inverse of off-frame ghosting, aimed inward at attention - turning a spatial instinct into a reusable mechanism
Uses :has() so an element containing the focus subject is exempted from receding, keeping ancestor chains visible
Reduced-motion path preserves the recede signal via desaturation while removing motion and blur
conventions: Knob pattern: data-* attributes ([data-focus]/[data-recede]) and an is-focusing class driving CSS vars
notes: A focus mechanism: mark a subject [data-focus] inside a .focus-scope (or use is-focusing) and the rest recedes (dim/desaturate/blur/lower) while the subject lifts; :has() spares ancestors and a reduced-motion fallback keeps only desaturation.
questions: Is is-focusing toggled by JS at runtime, and what drives the focus selection?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header describes bring-the-subject-forward / soften-the-rest as a mechanism, and the file implements both explicit and implicit forms plus a reduced-motion fallback.
apparent_themes: attention as a first-class spatial mechanism
inverse symmetry with off-frame ghosting (recede inward)
intention_signals: Aims to codify the instinct of focusing attention - lift the subject, push the rest back - into a reusable, tokenised CSS mechanism that works either by explicit marking or an implicit scope mode, while remaining accessible.
novelty: Casting attention/focus as a tokenised recede-and-lift mechanism (the inward inverse of off-frame ghosting), with :has()-based exemption of the subject's ancestors and a recede signal that survives reduced-motion as pure desaturation.
contribution: Contributes the focus/attention vocabulary: recede/lift tuning tokens, the .focus-scope container with explicit ([data-focus]/[data-recede]) and implicit (is-focusing) modes, and the reduced-motion fallback.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS file implementing a focus/attention mechanism via a .focus-scope container that lifts the [data-focus] subject and recedes (dims, desaturates, blurs, lowers) everything else, with explicit and implicit modes and a reduced-motion fallback.
intention_for_embedding: To turn the instinct of bringing one object forward while softening the rest into a reusable tokenised mechanism (the inward inverse of off-frame ghosting), usable by explicit marking or scope mode, and accessible under reduced motion.
